mnemonics.title: String Index Rules
mnemonics.content: NEVER use NEGATIVE index; MAX is LENGTH-1. Out of bounds throws EXCEPTION.

mnemonics.title: Substring Trap
mnemonics.content: Begin is INCLUSIVE, End is EXCLUSIVE - like a half-open interval [begin, end).

mnemonics.title: GetChars Usage
mnemonics.content: SRC_END is EXCLUSIVE (like substring); DST_BEGIN determines insertion point in destination array.

mnemonics.title: Immutability Benefit
mnemonics.content: SECURITY + SYNCHRONIZATION + HASH CACHING = Immutable Strings

acronyms.expansion: Negative Index Valid? - No, Exception thrown
acronyms.usage: Remember: negative string indices always cause exceptions

visualTips.visualization: Draw a row of boxes: index 0→'J', 1→'a', 2→'v', 3→'a'. Show that length=4 but last valid index is 3.
Show source string boxes being copied to destination array starting at dstBegin position, highlighting the offset.
Number line showing [begin, end) - filled circle at begin, empty circle at end, shaded region is the result.

analogies.analogy: Like array indexing - string is just an array of characters you can directly access using square bracket notation.
Like reading a book - you can look at it, copy passages, but can't write on the original pages.
Like cutting out a portion of a newspaper article - you get a new piece (substring) but the original remains intact.
